Anyway, I'm excited for this workshop. It's going to be a quick one-day workshop. Normally we do three, four or five-day workshops, and so you guys are lucky. You're just enough to keep your brains busy, not enough that you're going to be drowning in information like we normally do. So this will be a lot of fun. The theme of this seminar or this workshop today is how to go from six figures a year to seven figures a year in your business. And so for those who are just getting started, it's not going to be like, here's an A to Z to how to get started. If you're interested in more of that kind of stuff, look for our microcontinuity stuff that we have. That gives you a lot more of an A to Z type, step by step. This is kind of, you've got a business going how to take it to the next level. And even if you don't have a business yet, this is going to help just kind of get the thought processing ahead of how to build your business correct from the beginning to get you. So you can have a six-figure or excuse me, a seven-figure a year business. Because that's the kind of business I think all of us want, right? Most of us? No? Couple of you guys don't want that. All right. There are a lot of headaches come with a seven-figure business. I'm not going to lie. So some of you want to keep the six figures. I completely understand. So, but I'm glad to be here. I'm glad to have you guys here. I want to go through real quick and have everyone stand up and introduce themselves. It's because it makes it more fun if everyone gets to know each other. And at home, obviously, we can't introduce everybody. There's too many people. But we'll just start right here. And we have a microphone that you're not, you have the mic. Brenda, I'll grab it real quick. The mic doesn't hook to actual speakers at all, but this way the people at home can hear you. And it picks up on the speakers. Okay. Awesome. Thanks, you guys. I'm glad you're all here. We have a lot of stuff we're going to cover today in a very short period of time. So I'm going to jump right into it. Basically, this morning we've got two different things. I'm going to be covering kind of three core philosophies I wanted to go over with you guys. One I call the value ladder. One is about the lifeline of your customers, lifeline of a lead. And third is about your personality in your business to really dramatically increase your sales and your profits and everything else. And then after that, I'm going to have Mason Tanner, the guy that works with us. He's going to come down. He's going to be talking about media buying, which is, you know, a lot of guys are using the internet to drive traffic, build your business, make sales, that kind of stuff. But the problem with it is most people are using types of traffic generation, like let's say just affiliates. You get a big surge of sales and it kind of dies off. They're using paper click and it's getting harder and harder. There's a lot of stuff like that. And so we're, in our business, we're focusing a lot more on consistent type traffic, things that can bring traffic consistently day in and day out so you have predictable results. You know what you're going to make, how many people are going to come to your website, all those type of things. So Mason's going to be talking about media buying for probably an hour and a half to two hours today. Some really cool stuff he's doing with our business right now to see it more and more consistency with traffic. A lot of unique stuff people aren't really talking about, so it'll be fun. After that we're going to do lunch. I think Lindsay's bringing in Subway or something to have lunch. And then after lunch, Garrett, who runs our sales floor, he's going to be coming down and talking to everybody about back end sales. Because back end sales is, in our business, that's where we make most of our money. Obviously we generate a lot of money on the internet, but we generate a lot more money in the back end. In fact, in our company, we actually, our company Christmas Party on Saturday, we had a big huge contest. If you guys come through our offices, the downstairs, we have, I don't know, 7,000, 8,000 square feet, which is our whole call center and our coaching program. And the upstairs is all like the marketing and product development and that kind of stuff. So we have contests between the upstairs and the downstairs. And so the upstairs is basically all the money that's made on the internet, and the downstairs is all the money made on the back end off the internet. And that was the big competition is who can make more money this whole year. And we're going on a cruise, so that's kind of the fun thing. Whoever won the competition between upstairs and downstairs gets to get rooms with balconies and the people who lose have to be in the rooms like in the dungeon rooms where there's no windows and you're like getting sick to your stomach. And so it's been a lot of fun this whole year competing front end versus back end. And just to kind of prove my point really quick, the back end is beating the upstairs. And like I said, upstairs we do really, really well. The downstairs is beating us by about a little under a million dollars in sales. So there's a lot of money being made in the back end. So Garrison can come down and talk to you about how we're doing it. You guys have some ideas of how to build back ends into your programs you have as well. And then after that I'm going to spend some time with all your guys' businesses. We're going to write some up on the wall, get some ideas, look at the way they're structured right now, how to tweak some things, and that's about it. So it's going to be really fun. The interesting thing I found since I've been doing this business is there's not a lot of difference between a website or business that's making, you know, twenty, thirty thousand dollars a year and one that's making a million dollars a year. There's not a lot of huge shifts, huge change. It's a lot of little tiny things that most people are missing out on. It's getting consistent traffic. It's increasing your conversion rates. It's tweaking this, changing that, moving the sales process around a little bit. And that really makes a huge difference. It's not, oh crap, I need a new product. Oh, I need a change in my business model. It's not these huge things that most people think it is. It's usually very small tweaks, changes, ways you're communicating with your audience and that kind of stuff. So that's what we're covering today. We're going to be talking about some things to go to make a jump from a six-figure to a seven-figure-year business. Okay, the first thing I want to talk about, and this is a concept I like to call a value ladder. Now when I spoke, some of you guys know, we've been doing a lot of work with Tony Robbins and his company lately. And I had a chance, this summer, I got invited to Fiji to go out there and speak at their business mastery program, which is a program he sold business owners. It was ten thousand dollars ahead, plus they had to fly to Fiji and all that kind of stuff and pay for the resort. And Tony's got his own resort out there, which is just phenomenal. So two weeks before the workshop, they gave me a call. My wife and I were on our anniversary trip in Atlanta hanging out and we got a call from the secretary, saying, hey, do you want to come to Fiji in two weeks and come speak at Tony's business mastery? And like, it was the most horrible time in the world to possibly go drop everything. And we had our kids, we had a company party out in Vegas. We were flying everyone out to Vegas. We rented this place called Pirates Cove for a week. We were renting this place and basically we had to fly from Pirates Cove a day early all the way to Fiji. And we had our kids in Pirates Cove and there was like no way to get babysitters to get them back home. And I was on the phone and I was like, yep, I will be there. I don't know how we're going to make it, but we're going to be in Fiji in two weeks and I'll see you then. So we got off the phone and I'm like, crap. And so I'm going to sit around and I'm like, how in the world are we going to figure out how to get to Fiji? And so we're freaking out. And first thing we need is to figure out if we can get our kids there. So I called her back and I'm like, can we bring our kids? And they're like, well, we never had kids on the resort before. But sure, if you want to bring your kids, so I'm like, OK, good. So then I'm like, we got two weeks to get passports for three kids that are under three years old. I'm like, I don't know how that's even possible. So we went online and we found all these places to do like 24 hour passports. And we spent the next like week and a half just scrambling. And then we had a week at home and then we had to fly down to Vegas for our whole huge company party. So we're in Vegas trying to get our passports. And finally, the day before everything, we had to Fiji. We got the passports for the kids and everything showed up. We flew out just in time to make it to Fiji. But I got out there and I was ready to go and teach my normal internet marketing stuff to teach people how to create information business. And I got there and the group that was there, there were about 100 people, 100 business owners from Australia. And none of them were internet marketers. None of them had information products. They all had businesses. They were dentists. They were chiropractors. They were in the financial services. They were everything else in the world besides what I was used to teaching. And Tony's with there and he teaches for a day. And then if you guys know Chet Holmes, he speaks about sales for a whole day. And that's my turn. And I'm like, crap, I got to change everything. Everything I'm teaching right now, these guys, they don't want to create information product. And so I went back and I said, you know, what are the core concepts we teach business owners that in our business, it's good. But also our core principles for any business. And one of the biggest things we talked about with, I spent time with this group was talking about the value ladder. And this is where I think most businesses miss it out. Why they're most of them aren't making the kind of money that they should be. Whether it be, you know, get a dentist, a chiropractor, an internet marketer, a network marketer, it doesn't matter. They're missing this piece of the puzzle. So that's what we're going to talk about now is the value ladder. And this is kind of what it looks like. For those who've seen me draw this before, normally I have it on slides. I wanted to do a lot more talking about the concepts I wanted to do on a whiteboard. Okay, so basically in any business there's kind of a value ladder, right? People give money in exchange for value. Am I correct? How business usually works? And the more value you provide, the more money they're going to give you, right? That's kind of the way it all works out. And so the way that I look at business, I look at the value ladder like this. And right here at the bottom of your value ladder, this is where business usually starts. Someone's getting something in exchange for some kind of value, right? And the more, let me see if I can map this right. So this is money, right? And this is value. Value. So as the value increases, the money people will pay for the value, will pay, it will also increase as well. So create kind of a value ladder like this. It goes up to the top. Now for me and my business, the best thing we have, we do a three-day workshop called our partnership program. And I'm not sure if anyone in here has actually been to that workshop before. This is probably our best thing right here. It's a three-day workshop we do here in Boise. And during the workshop, we spend the first day, spend a full day with me going through a program like this, but breaking down business and all the internet marketing. We just rip the whole thing apart and rebuild it so everyone understands internet marketing. The second day, we break everything down into groups and you get a chance to work with my actual employees. So you get to spend an hour or 90 minutes with our copywriter, 90 minutes with our product development team, 90 minutes with our SEO guys, 90 minutes with our PPC guys, and 90 minutes with me going through your entire business, rebuilding and restructuring. So it's really, really good. And the third day, we come back down and it ever has a chance to whiteboard their business and map it out and have a really solid idea what their business should look like. And that's our best thing. We sell that for, I think, 11 or 12,000 bucks. And it's really expensive. In fact, we have programs hired now, but that's the one that I want. If I could have everyone who went through our programs come to that first, that would be the most important thing for me to send someone to. They get the most value and it's worth a lot of money. The problem is it's not that easy to go to somebody. You meet off the street and say, hey, I have this $12,000 program. Do you want to come to it next week? It's very, very difficult to sell that. I had a chance. I went to Tony Robbins' date with Destiny program last week. And it's a $10,000 program, excuse me, a $5,000 program. And it was amazing. They had 2,000 people that paid $5,000 ahead to be there. So if you do the math, that's $10 million in revenue from one seminar. But he can't even get people to show up at that seminar at the bat. They have a front-end seminar, a lower-end seminar that's like 500 bucks, called Unleash the Power Thin. People come to that and at that seminar, they upsell them to the higher-end seminar. So my business, this is the way it works. Again, I wish ideally I could sell someone this right at the bat, but it's difficult because they haven't seen the value. I haven't proved them. I have enough value for them to keep showing up and to pay that much money up front. So what I do is I bring them through an ascension model through a value ladder. Down here, I may give them, let's say, an article with some information. It's going to teach them some really cool stuff. Now, through the article, it's going to sell them on, hey, there's this really cool e-book I have that's going to teach you about this concept. Then they buy the e-book and they may upsell them to some videos. They buy the videos, they may buy like a webinar series. The webinar series may come and get coaching, and then a seminar, and then appear to our top-end programs. So from here, we go up, we have a mastermind group that's like 15K to 25K, and it keeps going up beyond there. So this is my ascension model, my business. So when somebody comes to my business, where do I lead someone at? I start down here, and I try to give value at this point. If they receive value, that person will say, man, that was really valuable. That was good. Good investment in my time, or my energy, or my money, whatever it is. I want more of that. And then they go and they seek the next thing, and the next thing, and they keep going up this value ladder. So when I first laid that out for this group in Fiji, a lot of them were like, well, that's a great Russell, but I don't sell e-books. I don't do seminars. I don't care about that kind of stuff. And I said, okay, let's break it down for some individual businesses. And I said, okay, who here, name out a business, show you how to business. So we had someone come, and they were a chiropractor. And so I said, let's build this value ladder for a chiropractor. Okay. And who here, is there any chiropractors here? Any friends, anyone's friends with chiropractors? Okay. Anyone with chiropractors making at least a million bucks a year, are you friends? Okay, this is why, most of them understand this process. The smart ones do. The smart ones are making bank because they understand this process. Okay, most chiropractors, where do they live and drive by? Where do they make their money? On the adjustment. How much they charge for adjustment? 90 bucks a month. 90 bucks a month, 40 bucks a time, something like that, okay? And that's their entire business. Now, chiropractors, how many years do they go to school to learn how to do this? Six. Okay, six years, they, six, seven years they go to learn how to do adjustment, right? Do you think that's all they learn in seven years? No. Okay, in fact, one of the buddies on my wrestling team, he, the chiropractors out of town for the week or something like that, and he needs adjustments. So we went on YouTube, he watched a video for like 15 minutes on how to do adjustments. He's like, okay, and he started adjusting all the other wrestlers, and he could adjust. After 15 minutes of watching YouTube video, he was able to do adjustment, okay? But they go to seven years of school to be able to do this, right? This is the mindset because these guys aren't business owners, they're chiropractors, they're going to do adjustments, and that's why they focus their business. But if you look at it, if you look at a value ladder, if they shift their thinking just a little bit, they can go dramatically from a, from a business to make in maybe six figures a year to one making seven figures a year just by shifting their value ladder, okay? So right here on the front end, let's say there's their $40 adjustment, okay? And then again, that's their business. If that's your business, you're kind of screwed, you're stuck at that point, right? Now if you're, if you're smart, and you're a smart chiropractor, you say, look, this is just the bottom rung of my ladder. This is how I'm getting someone in the door. I'm going to start giving away my adjustments for free or for really cheaper, whatever it might be. And then after I'm getting an adjustment, they come in and look, what's the next thing I can offer that person? What's the next logical thing? Okay? Now again, for most chiropractors, they look at it, hey, do you come in again next week for another adjustment? All they ever upsells, all they ever do. Okay, whereas if they could sell, they could sell packages, you know, hiring packages, or they could sell, you know, all their crap they're learning for 10 years of school, they could start taking that same information, they could bundle, they could create all sorts of different things with it. They could do wellness clinics, they could do, you know, rehab things. There's a thousand of things that they learned during their school that they know, the information they know. If they take that and they're restructuring and start adding different tiers, after you get your free adjustment, then hey, we're putting you on this program. You know, $200 a month where you get two adjustments and you get some nutrition training. And then you get this, and you get this, and they build a whole value ladder. Now this becomes a front-end lead generator. You have someone in the door with that, and everything else kind of builds along the way. Okay? One of my friends owns a lawn mowing company. His name's actually BJ Wright, he works upstairs as well. And he runs a lawn mowing company. And what was interesting, you see most lawn mowing companies out there, and what do they do? They charge $30 to come mow a lawn, right? So he went and said, look, every other ad out there saying, hey, I'll mow your lawn for $30. And so everybody know, I'll give you a free consultation and we'll mow your lawn for $30. So he said, well, crap, everybody else is doing that. Everyone else is mowing a lawn for $30. I'm going to give it away for free. So he did this thing where all his ads said, I'll come mow your lawn for free, and you start getting tons and tons of clients. You go to the mowing lawn for free. Now if they finish mowing their lawn, the person would come up with a little clipboard like this and say, hey, your lawn looks really, really good, but did you realize that you've got weeds that are growing in the back and your trees need trimming? And they start building a whole checklist of stuff they need to do for that person. They go up there and they upsell that person on a whole bunch of other stuff. You know, $600 package to come clean your yard and then $200 package to come and fix your sprinklers and then this and this and this and this. All these other products are then sold afterwards. The problem with most businesses is they look at their thing and that's their entire business. They don't realize that what most of us are doing, that's our front end of our business. That should not be our business. The ones that do this really well, the industry does this well, probably better than anyone out there. What you guys to be familiar with is the dental industry. I went through a process and I joked by Dennis about this all the time now because he did it so perfectly with me. But I came in for a free tooth whitening or for a, excuse me, a free tooth cleaning, teeth cleaning. I went in and did my teeth cleaning and he cleans my entire teeth and when I'm done I get a mouth that looks pretty good. And then when he's in there and he gets in and I sit up, he's like, Russell, you know, your teeth looking really good. There's a couple things that I noticed. Your teeth, they're getting a little yellow and it's not bad yet but it's going to get worse. And so what I can do actually with this teeth whitening kit, it's a little expensive but I can put you in a payment plan and for $2,000, $3,000 you can put you through this program and it'll whiten your teeth and it will give you trays that match your mouth and blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. And I'm like, oh, that sounds awesome. Okay, let's do it. So boom, it dings me for $2,500 there. And it's like, and the other thing I noticed, you know, when did you have braces? I'm like, why braces like 20 years ago? It's like, yeah, some of your teeth are starting to crowd again and start shifting. I don't know if you noticed that. I'm like, oh yeah, I noticed that. And it's like, when your teeth are shifting, you know, this is all the problems that come from you, you get headaches, you can get all these other things, you know, and I'm like, oh my gosh, this is a, you know, I'm freaking out at this point. And he's like, well don't worry, we can build you these retainers that will go in your mouth and it'll just keep your teeth straight. You just wear them at night time and they'll keep your teeth straight, they'll bring them back to the rat before. And it's only about $5,000. I'm like, oh crap, yes, I need that, please. And I went with my free teeth whitening and then all of a sudden, I spent almost $10,000 in fixing my teeth whitening and straightening. And my wife wanted the same thing to happen to her as well. Okay, we spent a fortune in the dentist because the dentist was smart. He realized that his business was not cleaning teeth. That's his front end that brings people in and everything else happens. All the profit happens back here in the rest of the value ladder. Okay, so every business should have a value ladder. If it doesn't, it's because you haven't thought about it long enough. Okay, and internet businesses are the easiest ones, I think, because everything kind of ascends really quickly, especially if you're in an information marketing business. You've got an e-book, a CD, and the value just kind of goes up just the way that things shipped around. Then the other concept, I'm going to come back to mine. So you go from article or video to free CD or DVD to a book, to a webinar series, to a workshop, to one-on-one coaching, whatever it is. So there's your model. The next concept that's really important to understand, and this is the concept, Evan Pagan kind of coined the term, the free line. I've never heard of that before, but figuring out where is your free line going to be at. Back when I first got started, the free line was right here. Everyone would give away, here's a free report, and that was kind of where everyone's free line was at. What happens, everyone's giving away so much free stuff like that, it's like, it's not that big a deal. Okay, again, my buddy in the lawn mowing industry, when he got started, everyone was giving away free consultation to come mow your lawn. He was like, well, crap, everyone's doing that. I need to move the free line. So he shifted it over and said, I'm going to give you a free lawn mowing. What happened is suddenly he started getting hundreds, not hundreds, but tons and tons and tons of accounts signing up because he'd shifted the free line over. So you have to look at your, after you build your value ladder out, you got to look at it and say, now where's my free line going to be at? Is it going to be back here? Is it going to be over here? Am I going to give away my book for free? Am I going to give away my webinar for free? Where's your free line going to be at? And that's where you're going to start bringing people in. Okay, well, obviously if this is going to cost you $2,000 to put on, you don't want to make your free line here. You don't want to move back here. You want to look at where's your free line going to be. Okay, for network marketing, let's talk about, I know we've got a bunch of network marketers in here. Let's map this out for network marketer. Okay, same kind of thing. Where, what, who's our network marketers in here? Okay, what are you guys selling? Coffee. Coffee? Something else. What are you selling? Agile. Agile? Agile. Okay, so what's your guys' product? What's your business? How are most of you guys doing this? I'm guessing. You're selling the product, right? No, actually I can't stop working with that because I got a whole bunch of people in Brazil and I'm kind of doing a lot of things. So we wanted to set up a new generation and bring new people in. So we have an ebook and some coaching and stuff like that. Okay, smart. Pause right there. Okay, so you, so, and this is where most internet network marketers make the mistake. Because they say, this is my product. So Agile is my product. And that's their entire business they're focusing on. You're doing it perfectly. As you said, we're bringing in an ebook which is dropping down the value ladder and we're bringing in coaching up here. Okay, so what just happened? Now he's got something here that he can do what with. Now he has a place to make a free line. I can give away an ebook. I can sell an ebook. I can do something with that. But see, now he's built the value ladder. Okay, let's say he wants to sell the book where I can drop down and I can make a video that I can give away for free. And there's my free line. So I give away everything for free. I sell the book. If I buy the book, now that this person is proven that I buy it, I can sell them into the company. And after they get into the company, crap, I'm going to sell them coaching. So they need to learn how to build a business. And I can make a whole bunch of money back here on the back end. Okay? Have you seen someone sell the coaching on the back end after you're paying them? Narrow marketing is difficult because most companies get pissed if you sell your own downline coaching. Yeah, I've been in many arguments with my company about that because I... But it's not that far. Yeah. So there's just... The industry makes this part difficult, but you can do it. It works. What's the kind of position it has you get access to coaching because you're doing it after it? Yeah, that's the way to shift RC, too. So... But there's a lot of things you do. You can make it a seminar. You can make an event. But having a value ladder in any business is huge. So what I want you guys to do right now is get your paper out and look at the business you're in right now. And I want you guys to create a value ladder. I want you to look at, first off, what's your product right now? Again, most people, I guarantee you've got a product and that's your business. Okay? So as soon as you understand that's not your business. That's one of the rungs on your value ladder. Then you can fill out everything prior to that and everything after that. Okay? We had a company that was here at our last partnership program. They do two or three million bucks a year. And I just explained just this concept. And afterwards, there's two older gentlemen. The guy raised his hand. Excuse me. He said, Russell, this is worth the entire trip. He's like, I didn't realize that our business was a value ladder. He's like, we've had two or three things in here to kind of do. He's like, now that I can see it like that, I was able to map out four or five points prior and later. And from there, I restructured my entire business. Now I have just something I can do with my entire business. Okay? It's a simple concept. It's not difficult. When you understand this, everything we're talking about next step, which is going to be laying out your lifeline of your lead. These concepts, this concept is very important for you to understand for the next step of it. Okay? So it's going to be five minutes mapping out what your value ladder is going to look like. Scott, can you play music real quick while everyone's mapping it out? So you know, it's an MLM is a value ladder the way they put people through and you send up as, oh, now you're a gold superstar person. And then now you're a diamond eagle person. They want it to fill transparent and generic. And so what I'm thinking of doing is every so often opening and doing like a launch and saying, OK, there's an opportunity. There's 20 spots to join our down line and doing like a product launch to be a part of our down line. So take the leads from just kind of the info business and bring them in. And then I would have, I think maybe just three levels. So just like a general just to join and then get access to standard training, which would be like weekly webinars and like a training portal, like a mini membership site. And then the next level I'm thinking is a commitment to join with the co-op. So we'll have like the marketing funnel drives everything. Really, it's not that hard to do. They just have to call their lead. And so we just make some sort of a commitment as far as how much they're going to spend. And I don't know what that would be. Maybe it's like a hundred a month or 500 a month on there, depending on where they came in. This would also be like they have to join at the highest level. And then the next thing I came up with a while ago, a program where if it was like a business owner that wanted to generate another stream of income, they could come in and they could come in at the high level and they could come in a thousand dollars for ads. And basically the whole idea was to get 10 people to do the same thing. So they can MLM with them. They have one. So like right now I have a lady named Dawn and she wants to do a site called the MLM Moms. If they're willing to kind of work, take an equity or a cut of the business. So that's kind of what I'm thinking after the lead generation one. Very cool. I like that a lot. Very cool. All right. Well, cool. We actually kind of the process, how that works. And really like spending a lot of time, especially if you're starting a new business or if you're looking at business, realizing from the get go, this is the biggest mistake most business owners make as they get started is like, holy crap, Russell, I've got an idea. I'm making a product that teaches people how to do this. And that's, and there's so focused on this is the business, this product. And there's a big separation between a product and a business. Okay, a product is Arm and Hammer or Head and Shoulders, Dander Shampoo, right? That's a product. Okay, you think Procter and Gamble, look at that. Is that their business? No. Procter and Gamble, someone comes by Head and Shoulders, they're, hey, that person might also want toothpaste, they might also want this and this. And they build the whole product line. Okay, they build the business. Okay, same thing. If you look at your book, whatever it is you're selling as your business, okay, you missed out on the entire business. Okay, a product and a business are two separate things and you have to have a value ladder to be able to call the business. Okay, it's knowing, it's realizing that. And when you define this whole thing, you can map it out. In fact, when I was in Fiji, when I asked the question of a hundred business owners, I think it was like one person, maybe two that had, I never thought about like a value ladder. Like, where am I taking my people to? What's the process? What's my goal with this person? Okay, my goal is to get people into my $25,000 mastermind. If I realize that from the get go, everything else I'm doing is going to be structured towards that. Okay, if you come to one of our, one of our two day workshops, two day workshop, I'm always talking about the mastermind experience, the mastermind experience. And guess what? The third day of the two and a half day workshop is a mastermind experience. People come down to mastermind their business out there. Okay, now for me to come and say, Hey, if you guys like this mastermind experience, can we have something like this that's only $25,000 a year? Okay, it's very logical step now. Holy crap, that is cool. I want to be part of that experience. Okay, everything is leading to the next thing through the value ladder. Okay, a dentist knows he's not trying to clean teeth. He's trying to whiten. He's trying to straighten. He's trying to do all these other things. He's leading someone continually through their value ladder. Okay, and the way you're going to structure your products can be a lot different when you realize that there's a value ladder versus, Hey, I have a Z book. I have a CD series. I have this webinar series. If you realize that there's a value ladder and you've defined that in your head, even if this part's not built yet, you know, eventually you want to get to there. You build it differently because you understand that it's a process. You're moving someone from one place to the next to the next and you can prepare and get people ready at every step along on the chain. Okay, so that's it for value ladder. Any questions or comments or anything about that at all? Okay, that's a core concept that's very important to understand, especially for the next thing we're going to be talking about, which is where I get more excited. Next part's really fun for me. Okay, the next thing we're going to talk about is what I like to call the lifeline of a lead. Okay, so all of us are obviously we're all in different businesses, but every business lives and dies by leads, right, by customers, by people that are coming in. There's very few, I don't think I've ever seen a business doesn't live off of customers. I think there could be any situation where there's not a customer involved. One of our workshops is awesome. This lady came in and we were talking about stuff and she raised her hand and she was just like, but also I just don't want to sell anything. Is there any way I can make money online without selling anything? I'm like, well, someone's got to buy something for you to make money. That's just kind of the way it all works. So it's kind of a funny thing, but every customer needs leads. And when you understand there's a path that everyone in your leads is going to go through and again, it ties back to the whole value ladder, but there's a pathway that people are going to come along. We understand that path. You can look at it and see, you're aware of it. You can start looking at it and making a lot of decisions on what you're doing based on that path. So for example, I'm going to map out a really quick one and you guys are going to see kind of the outside of what it looks like and I'm going to dissect all the pieces internally. So you see what I'm looking at when I'm building a business. So for example, we're talking about microcontinuity. A lot of you guys have gone through that program and know a little bit about it. We're driving traffic from a lot of different systems, a lot of different places we're driving traffic from. They're coming to a landing page. On this landing page, we're asking for the name and email address. Sometimes they're phone depending. And on this page, I'm usually giving away a free video revealing what? Number one secret to something. So come to that page, you give the name and email address immediately afterwards, I'm taking to a page where I'm trying to sell them something. Let's say free CD. They're paying 495 shipping. And you get in the CD after they buy the CD, some depending on the program, I'm putting one on a continuity program. Let's say where every, every month I'm billing them, I'm delivering something every month. I'm billing them something. So this is kind of a weekly program or a monthly program. But after they purchase this, then I'm going to take them to usually an upsell chain. And the way I normally do mine is all maybe do a $97 upsell. If they say no to that, I'll give them a $47 down sell. And then I'll offer them $197 upsell to say to note of that, I might offer them a $97 down sell. And then from there, I might do $497 upsell if they say no, $249. And then from there, I'll let them into the members area, right? And then from there, if you know my process at all, they get a letter in the mail that's trying to get them to call a phone number. And if they don't call a phone number, then I call them on the phone right here. We offer them a $5,000 up to, I think the biggest one I've sold so far is $65,000 package on the phone. Or if they get this letter, they're calling in and we're offering the same kind of coaching program, which takes them to a seminar. And from the seminar, then if they come to the partnership level, I'm trying to upsell them to a mastermind level, which is between $15 and $25,000 a year. And that's kind of the way my sales process mapped out. Okay. It's pretty fun. So let me walk you through. And I've had different sales processes. That's kind of the core one we do a lot in our business. Okay. Now every business obviously is going to be different. I'm going to map you out a couple of different things, but I want to walk you through because a lot of people, you've probably seen this before, right? You come to our two-day, our two and a half day workshop, but at the time everyone's done there, able to sit there and map out, this is my business model. It's going to look like that's kind of our goal after the two and a half days. But there are a lot of little things that we don't have time to talk about in that workshop that would go into the building and planning and thought process behind how this whole thing works. Okay. So I'm going to start clear back here prior to any traffic being sent. Okay. Prior traffic being sent, somebody has to place an ad somewhere, right? All traffic comes off of an ad. You guys agree with that? Even SEO, you agree with that? Okay. Still coming off an ad, right? Okay. All traffic's coming from some kind of ad originally. Okay. Now there's a lot of thought process that goes into any type of ad. Most people throw up an ad and then that's the last thing to think about. Throw up an admin and they're like, Hey, people aren't buying. What's going on? What's going on? Okay. It starts way back here at the ad. If you're not thinking about the ad, that level, you're missing a huge, huge, huge part of it. Okay. So the first thing we'll talk about is a concept called pre-framing. And even here, we learned about NLP at all, NeuroLongistics Program. I mean, if you not go buy every book on it, it's like the most fascinating topic in the world. Um, the NLP is the whole study about how humans behave, how the brains work and how much really, really cool stuff. And people have written a lot of really cool books about how to use NLP for sales, for marketing, a lot of different stuff. But the pre-framing, just framing it as a concept in its whole is just one of the coolest things in the world. But using it for selling is even really cool, is even cooler. So pre-framing, for example, let's say, um, let's say, so a good example, they might teach you NLP. So let's say, um, someone comes to me and they, they want to argue with me about something. They have, they have, we have a confrontation, right? So someone's going to come to me and say, Russell, I think that, um, I think that your way you drive, your idea about internet marketing is horrible. I think you suck. And I hope you die. Okay, they come to me with that right there. Okay, now I'm going to be responding back to that person. Now there's two different things I can do. First thing I can say is I can kind of come, become confrontational and say, you think it, what? Okay, so they do that, what happens to that person? You think I suck at this and I start, start going that direction, right? With, with my, with my conversation. And me, that person, because of the way that my first sentence came out, they now are ready to start attacking back, right? Cause that's the way it came. Okay, now the same conversations happen. The person starts attacking me, tell me how they don't like something about me. And the first thing I come out is, you know what I agree with you. But the reality is, and I go into my thing, but all I do is I say, I agree with them. Guess what just happened? I just set a pre frame from the rest of my sentence. Soon as I said, I agree with you. That doesn't matter if I don't agree with that person at all. Everything I say after that point, that person in their mind, they just said, this person agrees with me. And all of a sudden they now are, are putting themselves in a place where they're like, I want to listen to this guy. I just say, because he agrees with me. I want to know he has to say because, and this, it completely shifts the, the way that that person starts listening to the rest of the sentence. Whereas if I say, I disagree with you or I'm angry at you or anything else I say, as soon as I say that, that person, now they don't need, most people don't even hear what you say. If you notice that argument, people don't hear what you say. This all comes down to the way you're framing things. And so if I pre frame, hey, I really agree with you. But the reality is, and even if I'm not agree with any point they say, I just say, I agree with you. Some of that person's, all their, their, everything, all their, um, all their guards go down. Everything changes and the whole conversation changes just by saying, I agree with you, even if I don't agree with you. Okay. So there's a pre frame I'm structuring right before that sentence. Okay. If you look at ourselves guys, they pre frame a lot of their sentences. A lot of times if you see me sell from the stage, I pre frame a lot of things I'm saying, the words I'm using when I'm saying things, I'm using pre frames to set someone in the right state of mind that I want them to be in for the rest of the presentation. Okay. The same thing happens with an ad. Okay. So for the most basic dumb explanation, I heard this from Mark, Mark joined her a long time ago. He said, if, if there are two ads, both of them are driving the same website. And this ad right here says, Russell Brunson is a scam artist. He stole my money. I hope he dies. He's a horrible person. I don't like his wife or kids either. And I hate his religion, but he's got this product. Go check it out. Okay. So I'm going to click on that ad and they go here. What's going to happen to hit this page? Not a lot. Right. They're not going to buy my product. Okay. If the ad or the page come right here and it says, Russell Brunson amazing. He changed my life because of this, because of his teaching, I was able to change my life. My business, I was able to make money, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. Here's this book he's got out here. You should go check it out. Click here. And it goes the same page. What's going to happen? Okay. It's going to be night and day. And the ad is the same. The sales letter is exactly the same. It's the frame that I sent that person through before they got there. Just like my argument, if I pre-frame the sentence right before I start talking to you, it changes the whole motion of it. Same thing with an ad. Did you have a question? I'm just going to say that's why I bought my company. I'm going to tell you because I saw a banner on someone's blog that was about, it's kind of like a four hour week style ad about how you were doing your dirty little wrestler or whatever. And because I'd frame you that way, I was interested. Otherwise, I would have been. Very cool. Very cool. So he went through one of our pre-frames, so it works. He's living proof this works. All right. Okay. Now, obviously, most things are not going to be that subtle normally, right? They're going to give me a lot more subtle normally. They're not going to be huge shifts like this. If you guys remember, for those who were following this industry very much, a little while ago, something happened. These things came out. They were called flogs. F-L-O-G-S. Sounds for fake blogs. The FCK came and just kicked the crap out of everyone who was using them. Very unethical. Very bad way to do business. But I want to show you some of the results of people that I know that were using these things. Okay, it's a blog. And they'd say, this is Cindy's diet blog. It is Cindy. And she's like this huge lady, and then she's skinny right afterwards. And it's a blog that looks like it's Cindy's personal blog, like on Blogger or something like that. Or on Blogspot. And she's posting this blog about how much weight she lost, how great it is. And she did it by taking these two supplements. This Acai Berry and this Kolen Cleanser. If you get these things, just like me, you can go from fat to skinny in like two seconds. And people put these flogs up there, and they started driving people to this page. And it doesn't look like an ad at all. It looks like someone's personal blog. And then there's like, oh, by the way, these are two products I took to make me lose all this weight. And they go on, right? And so look at that. You're like, that's not an ad. That's someone's blog. And people are driving all these traffic to these fake blogs. But what happened was just amazing. People who had conversion rates, like one to 3% over here on the cells there, would push them through a flog like this, and would shoot them up to like 6% to 7%. And people would be buying two products as opposed to one. One of my friends that was running a bunch of these, he said that on the first product, they were getting like two times the order they were if they just took it directly here. And then whatever they purchased right here, 50% would also buy the second one. So they're getting one time. So basically when you looked at driving traffic through a flog to your offer or your multiple offers, like the amount of money was like 10-fold what it was just driving strictly to the sales page. And again, it comes down to what's the pre-frame. Now obviously these are unethical, illegal, and you shouldn't do them. But think about the concept real quick. Think about the concept. It's just a pre-frame you're sending someone through before you get there. So how do you do your ads? And the ads are changing a lot because the FTC, for anyone who's following that at all, is coming down on using case studies or testimonials, that kind of stuff. So prior to December 1st, when the law has changed, we used to do a lot of ads, probably like the ones you saw, which are very either, here's a story about someone that had to do something, push them to a thing, or here's how this guy's trying to live his dreams of wrestling, or whatever it might be. And we're positioning these ads very strategically, and they're worded very strategically, to put someone through a frame so that when they show up to our site, they're more likely to purchase. Because anyone can tell you that if you go to Google and you click on an ad, in their mind, they're saying this is an ad. But if I can put things in other places that don't look like ads, but they're pre-framing, and they're putting someone in the right position, when they show up to my site, that person's going to be much, much more likely to buy. So always think about that from this standpoint. When you're doing your initial lead gen, your initial marketing, look at the frame you're sending somebody to your website to. If you're doing seminars, the frame they're coming to your seminar through. A lot of times, especially network marketing guys, kill us, because they'll do these big, huge events, you know, come to the party, and they never even mentioned that there's going to be network marketing. It's like, hey, come to this thing, it's going to be free lunch and dinner. People are like, sweet, I'm in, I'm coming to free lunch and dinner. Who are the people that want to go to free lunch and dinner? Normally, they're people that are completely broke, right? But the frame they sent them to is, hey, come get free lunch and dinner. They sit them down in a room, they sit them down, and then they sit there and they pitch them for like four hours. Okay? That person, the frame they came to, it was horrible. I'm here for free lunch, now you're trying to sell me crap? Okay, the frame that they're coming through is just not ideal to make the selling situation easier. Okay, so it's all about how do you pre-frame the thing to get them to wherever you're trying to get them to. Any questions or comments about pre-framing? This stuff fascinates me. You just may or think of something that's your own response. Let's do this too so we can get people to help. Is that on, Scott? Yes, it is. You just made me think of something where indirect response, though, is talk about message to market match. But within your market, you have a message to mindset match, and that's the pre-frame. Yep.
